feed.title: Google Cloud: Migrate to Virtual Machines
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Migrate to Virtual Machines

entry.content: Migrate to Virtual Machines now lets you use regional disks for the target virtual machine (VM) instance.
entry.summary: Migrate to Virtual Machines now supports regional disks for your target VM instances. This enhancement allows for improved availability and resilience during your migrations.
entry.title: Feature: Regional Disks for VM Migration

entry.content:
Migrate to Virtual Machines now supports the following operating systems for VMware, AWS, Azure, and image import sources:

  • Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10
  • CentOS 10
  • AlmaLinux 10
  • Oracle Linux 10
  • Rocky Linux 10

CentOS is not supported on ARM architecture.
entry.summary: Migrate to Virtual Machines now supports RHEL 10, CentOS 10, AlmaLinux 10, Oracle Linux 10, and Rocky Linux 10 for VMware, AWS, Azure, and image import. Note that CentOS 10 is not supported on ARM architecture.
entry.title: Feature: New OS support for Migrate to Virtual Machines

entry.content: You can now use Hyperdisk Storage Pools with migrating VMs if you need large-scale storage. Hyperdisk Storage Pools help you manage large-scale storage and can optimize costs and performance.
entry.summary: Introducing Hyperdisk Storage Pools for migrating VMs, enabling efficient large-scale storage management to optimize costs and performance.
entry.title: Feature: Hyperdisk Storage Pools for Large-Scale VM Migration

entry.content: Migrate to Virtual Machines now supports all available versions of AlmaLinux EL 8 and 9.
entry.summary: Migrate to Virtual Machines now supports all available versions of AlmaLinux EL 8 and 9.
entry.title: Feature: AlmaLinux Support Extended

entry.content: Generally available: The Basic Input/Output System (BIOS) to Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) conversion option is now generally available. Migrate to Virtual Machines lets you convert the OS boot type of a VM instance from BIOS to UEFI. This option is useful when you want to securely boot your VM instance, as secure boot is only supported by UEFI. For more information, see the table in Configure the target for a migrated VM.
entry.summary: The conversion of BIOS to UEFI boot type for VM instances is now generally available. This feature is beneficial for enabling secure boot, which requires UEFI.
entry.title: Feature: BIOS to UEFI Conversion Now Available

entry.content: Starting with version Migrate Connector 2.7, throttling is supported for a second NIC.
entry.summary: Starting with version 2.7, the Migrate Connector supports throttling for a second network interface.
entry.title: Feature: Throttling Support for Second NIC in Migrate Connector
